spec.title: Projects card grid, KPI summary, lead conversion flag
spec.context:
The projects page currently renders only the generic AG Grid SSRM table (domain `projects`). The user wants a card grid as the DEFAULT view: 4 KPI tiles on top, then a responsive grid of project cards with infinite scroll. The AG Grid table stays reachable through a view toggle (filters / saved views / export are not lost).
The reference mock shows a per-card conversion rate. That data did not exist: leads had no "converted" flag and Project had no path to leads except Project -> campaigns -> leads. The user approved adding the flag (in scope of this spec).
spec.amendment: The user revoked the lead conversion flag: `leads.is_converted` must not exist in the migration, in the lead form or in the leads table. Everything built on top of it is removed with it — the per-card and global conversion rate, the `converted` / `conversion_rate` stat widgets of the `leads` and `projects` modules, the `converted_leads_count` aggregate. REVOKED by this amendment: D-1, BR-1, BR-3, the leads endpoint change in the data contract, AC-004, AC-006, and the conversion part of AC-001. The "exactly 4 leading stat widgets per module" invariant (spec 0026-module-stats-panel) is preserved by replacing the removed counters: `leads` now exposes total / assigned / with_source / with_site, `projects` exposes total / campaigns / leads / allocated_budget (sum of the linked campaigns' total_budget, currency format). `App\Support\ConversionRate` survives: percentStat still serves `registries` and `companies`.

decisions.d: REVOKED (see amendment). Was: lead conversion is a single boolean column `leads.is_converted` (default false, indexed).

Pagination for the card grid follows the existing codebase convention (offset/limit + `paginatedResponse` envelope, as for-select and notifications). No cursor, no page/per_page: those exist nowhere in this backend.
The card grid is a NEW plain index endpoint `GET /api/projects`, not the table framework: card payload != table row payload. The table framework stays untouched.
Project reaches leads via `hasManyThrough(Lead, Campaign)`. No direct FK.

business_rules.br: REVOKED (see amendment): no conversion rate is computed or exposed anywhere for projects or leads.

Every new endpoint is authorized server-side with `projects.viewAny` (ProjectPolicy). Per-card action affordances are gated by `can.update` / `can.delete`, computed server-side with the Gate; the UI only hides.
REVOKED (see amendment): `is_converted` no longer exists, so it is no longer a field of the leads authorization resource.

endpoint.name: projects.index
endpoint.request: search?: string (max 100, matches code or name) offset?: int >= 0 (default 0) limit?: int 1..60 (default 12) project_status_id?: int (exists:project_statuses,id)
endpoint.response: { items: [{ id: int, code: string, // "PRJ-0001" name: string, description: string|null, project_status: { id, name, color: string|null } | null, campaigns_count: int, leads_count: int, converted_leads_count: int, conversion_rate: int|null, // BR-1 total_budget: string|null, allocated_budget: string, remaining_budget: string|null, start_date: string|null, // Y-m-d end_date: string|null, can: { update: bool, delete: bool } // BR-2 }], export_link: null, pagination: { total, offset, limit, total_pages } }

endpoint.name: projects.summary

endpoint.change: REVOKED (see amendment): the leads contract is unchanged by this spec. No `is_converted` field exists on the request, the resource, the table or the meta.

acceptance_criteria.ac: GET /api/projects returns projects ordered by created_at desc, paginated with offset/limit, each item carrying campaigns_count and leads_count. (Amended: no converted_leads_count, no conversion_rate.)
GET /api/projects with `search` matches on code OR name; with `project_status_id` filters by status.
Both new endpoints return 403 for a user without `projects.viewAny`.
`can.update` / `can.delete` reflect the real Gate result per user.
REVOKED (see amendment): no lead payload, table column or form field carries `is_converted`.

The projects page shows 4 KPI tiles fed by /api/projects/summary and a responsive card grid (1 / 2 / 3 columns at 375 / 768 / 1024+) that loads the next page when the bottom sentinel becomes visible (infinite scroll), with skeletons while fetching.
A view toggle switches between the card grid (default) and the existing AG Grid table; the "Nuovo progetto" button stays available in both.
Each card shows code, status badge, name, the 3 stats (campaigns / leads / conversion) and an "Apri scheda" link to /projects/{id}; edit affordance only when can.update.
